channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Are Your GitHub Stats Worthy of a FIFA Card?
item.description: Are you a football fan? Since the FIFA hype is at its absolute peak at this moment, it is hard to ignore. We have Erling Haaland single-handedly carrying Norway toward the finals, and we even have...

item.title: CPIs and PDA Signers: What I Built and What Actually Clicked
item.description: I spent four days building programs that move SOL and tokens on Solana. Day 71 was a simple SOL transfer through a CPI. Day 74 was a mint whose authority belongs to a PDA with no private key —...

item.title: Amazon updated 2023’s Fire HD 10 tablet with 4GB of RAM
item.description: The Fire HD 8 that launched in 2024 was the last new addition to Amazon's budget-minded tablet lineup, but the company has quietly updated the Fire HD 10 that debuted the year before. In 2023 it...

item.title: Build Your First AI Agent in 30 Minutes - CrewAI + AWS Bedrock
item.description: 👋 Hey there, Tech Enthusiasts! I'm Sarvar, a Cloud Architect who loves turning complex tech problems into simple solutions. I've worked with AWS, Azure, DevOps, Data, Analytics, Generative-AI and...

item.title: AI Agent Observability Runs on Conversation IDs | Focused Labs
item.description: Agent observability gets weirdly polite at the exact moment it should get nosy. It records the model call, stores the prompt, counts tokens, and then loses interest right when the agent starts...

item.title: I replaced 1,000 lines of Python with a 500-word prompt
item.description: Or: what building a $0/month autonomous AI librarian taught me about running LLM agents in production. My documentation wiki was rotting. Not dramatically — just the usual entropy: pages with no...

item.title: What Ball Tracking Misses in Tennis Video Analysis
item.description: Most tennis video analysis starts with the ball. Where did it land? How fast did it move? Was the shot in or out? What was the trajectory? Those are useful questions. But they are not the whole...

item.title: NestJS Zero-Downtime Deployment on DigitalOcean with GitLab CI/CD and PM2
item.description: Deploying a NestJS backend to production with true zero downtime is more involved than most guides suggest. This is a complete, battle-tested walkthrough — built from real production experience —...

item.title: Why I'm Building AI in Public (And Why This Time Is Different)
item.description: Why I'm Building AI in Public (And Why This Time Is Different) For the last few months, I've been learning, experimenting, and building with AI. Like many developers, I spent a lot of time...

item.title: I Pointed My AI Research Engine at Goldbach's Conjecture — It Found a Hidden Bias (2026)
item.description: As a developer building AI for scientific discovery, I wanted to test if autonomous research actually works. So I built Luka and pointed it at Goldbach's conjecture. The Background Goldbach's...

item.title: I Built an AI Research Engine and It Found a Perfect Power Law in Twin Primes (2026)
item.description: I'm a developer, not a number theorist. But I built Luka — an autonomous AI research engine — and pointed it at one of math's oldest open problems. What it found blew my mind. How It Started I'm a...

item.title: Google DeepMind Unionization Talks Are Off to a Rocky Start
item.description: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.

item.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description: Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft. According to...

item.title: While you’re watching the World Cup, the feds may be watching you
item.description: It's a big year for America. It's the semiquincentennial, otherwise known as America250, and the United States is cohosting the World Cup. But spectators at these events - and the millions of...

item.title: This slim camera has a transparent LCD screen for a viewfinder
item.description: Despite the fact that smartphones have become impressively capable shooters, standalone point-and-shoot cameras are enjoying a renaissance. The tiny Kodak Charmera is still wildly popular, while...

item.title: I finally got my Trump phone
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. One year on, our phones have finally arrived. 12 months, 16 days, 21 hours, and 54 minutes after I first heard about Trump...

item.title: Hydration isn’t complicated: Just drink water
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
item.description: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one...

item.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and...

item.title: Jon Prosser responds to Apple lawsuit by blaming the other guy
item.description: YouTuber Jon Prosser has finally filed a formal response to Apple's lawsuit made against him and another defendant over allegedly stealing iOS secrets. In his response, Prosser denied that he...

item.title: The Verge’s annual summer ‘in’ and ‘out’ list
item.description: In the AI slop-loaded, algorithm-powered modern reality, trends come and go - and the tech industry is no different. For the last few years, The Verge staff has compiled a selection of things that...

item.title: The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m...

item.title: Apple TV is hitting its stride
item.description: Since its inception, Apple TV, née Apple TV Plus, has built a reputation on quality over quantity. It has far fewer shows and movies than the likes of Netflix or Disney Plus, but generally...

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
item.description: Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. The AI startup, best known for generating images, released a behind-the-scenes video of its...

item.title: Flock Cameras Can Surveil Cars Without License Plates
item.description: This is from a 2024 company presentation: Officers can also tap into data showing a car’s decals, bumper stickers, back and top racks—along with temporary and unique state tags. Flock calls it a...

item.title: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
item.description: A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while...

item.title: The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway.
item.description: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. The five-year-old is given internet-based...

item.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data. The stealer, discovered...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
item.description: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

item.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
item.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.
